title: Examples: drawit()
text: drawit() designed mimic New York Times “Draw ” tool, viewers shown part graph asked predict trend full data revealed. Earlier versions youdrawitR package required users work JavaScript D3. version requires familiarity ggplot2. vignette, demonstrate drawit() tool variety circumstances, using real simulated data. Although package originally created graphical testing, can used broadly setting users want sketch draw graph.

title: Examples: drawit()
text: geom_point geom_smooth geom_line , pass plot drawit(), either pipeline saving object first output display graph yellow box. Click inside box move left right draw prediction. move, line appear. yellow box indicates section graph still needs completed, make sure draw across full highlighted region.

title: Examples: drawit()
text: drawit() tries match x-value original data. especially helpful want collect user-drawn values merge back plotted data, potentially useful measure distance original x-point user-drawn y-point. However, situations may useful loosen x-y connection. Let’s see used mtcars. smoothness chunkiness drawn path depends densely packed data . Compare mtcars example palmerpenguins dataset. example, drawn path much jittery requires precision mouse movement connect smoothly. Moving mouse slowly across plot often helps function capture movement accurately. jitteriness becomes problem, consider reducing density data increasing rendered height width plot. Another option use smoother parameter, often makes interaction easier control. Notice drawn line becomes smoother smoother value increases 0. happens nearby x-values likely grouped together, reduces small, jittery movements drawing. can make interaction feel easier controlled, may also reduce precision recording user data, resulting x-values longer align closely original data. Choosing appropriate smoothing value depends goal. need point--point comparisons, lower value appropriate. interested overall trend, want make drawing experience less sensitive small mouse movements, higher value can helpful. need flexibility, check sketchit(). function freeform can especially useful data dense.
code: (ggplot(data = mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) +   geom_point()) |>    drawit() penguins_adelie <- dplyr::filter(penguins, species == "Adelie")  (ggplot(data = penguins_adelie, aes(x = bill_length_mm, y = bill_depth_mm)) +   geom_point()) |>    drawit() (ggplot(data = penguins_adelie, aes(x = bill_length_mm, y = bill_depth_mm)) +   geom_point()) |>    drawit(smoother = 0.5)

title: Examples: drawit()
text: drawit() perfect replica ggplot2, preserve much styling information ggplot object pass . example , line color, line width, labels, axis limits reflected interactive version. However, render ggplot, features may appear exactly expected. youdrawitR still work progress, certain plot elements may yet fully supported may render differently original ggplot2 object.
code: (ggplot(data = mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) +     geom_line(linewidth = 2, color = "red") +     labs(title = "Drawit Cars Example",          subtitle = "Hope You Enjoy!",          x = "Weight",          y = "Miles Per Gallon") +    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 32))+    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1.5, 7))  ) |>    drawit(smoother = 0.2)

title: Examples: drawit()
text: drawit() especially useful simulated data forecasting-style tasks, since originally designed graphical testing. function includes several arguments let control parts plot shown user, drawing begins, revealed interaction complete. Often, helpful ask users continue trend beyond observed data. can control drawing region begins draw_start argument drawit(). drawit() supports two geoms time. example, can include true line starting point user guide continuing pattern. Alternatively, can reveal full line user finishes drawing using show_on_finish argument. show_on_finish set TRUE, drawit() use secondary plot “revealed” plot.
code: # Here is the simulated data we will be using data = tibble(x = seq(1, 25, .5),                y = exp((x-15)/30),               ypoints = exp(((x-15)/30) + rnorm(30, 0, 0.1))) #> Warning in ((x - 15)/30) + rnorm(30, 0, 0.1): longer object length is not a #> multiple of shorter object length (ggplot(data, aes(x, ypoints)) +   geom_point(data = data |>  mutate(ypoints2 = ifelse(x < 10, ypoints, NA)),              aes(y = ypoints2),              size = 2,               colour = "magenta"              ) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.5, 1.5)) +   labs(x = "X-Label", y = "Y-Label")) |>   drawit(draw_start = 10) (ggplot(data, aes(x, ypoints)) +   geom_point(data = data |>  mutate(ypoints2 = ifelse(x < 10, ypoints, NA)),              aes(y = ypoints2),              size = 2,               colour = "magenta"              ) +    geom_line(data = data |>  mutate(y = ifelse(x < 10, y, NA)),             aes(y = y)) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.5, 1.5)) +   labs(x = "X-Label", y = "Y-Label")) |>   drawit(draw_start = 10) (ggplot(data, aes(x, ypoints)) +   geom_point(data = data |>  mutate(ypoints2 = ifelse(x < 10, ypoints, NA)),              aes(y = ypoints2),              size = 2,               colour = "magenta"              ) +    geom_line(data = data,             aes(y = y)) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.5, 1.5)) +   labs(x = "X-Label", y = "Y-Label")) |>   drawit(draw_start = 10, show_on_finish = TRUE)

title: Examples: drawit()
text: drawit() function integrates directly Shiny, enabling interactive drawing inputs captured reused within reactive workflows. used inside Shiny application, drawit() behaves differently static contexts. Instead returning r2d3 widget, returns list, contains: youdrawit_plot: interactive drawing widget points: reactive tibble containing user’s drawn data points object reactive() expression resolves tibble : x: x-values corresponding draw space y: corresponding user-drawn values enable communication browser Shiny server, must supply shiny_message_loc argument using drawit() Shiny. argument defines input name used send drawn data browser back Shiny. drawing interaction handled JavaScript, Shiny runs R server, shiny_message_loc acts bridge two.

title: Examples: drawit()
text: following example demonstrates minimal Shiny app captures user-drawn points returns tibble user finishes drawing (yellow box completely filled ), res$points() returns tibble one row x-value drawable region. might look something like : exact number rows depend x-values original plot user’s drawing. resulting x y values similar scale original dataset, allowing easy merges comparisons.
code: library(shiny)  # Define the User Interface (ui) ui <- fluidPage(   # Placeholder for the interactive drawit widget   uiOutput("widget_ui") )  # Define the Server Logic server <- function(input, output, session) {   # Create a ggplot object as the base plot for drawing   p <- ggplot(data = penguins, aes(x = bill_length_mm, y = bill_depth_mm)) +     geom_point(size = 2)      # This is the "bridge" between JavaScript (browser) and Shiny (R server)   shiny_message_loc <- "scatter_points"      # Initialize drawit    res <- drawit(     p, # the ggplot object     smoother = 2,     shiny_message_loc = shiny_message_loc     )   # res contains:     # - youdrawit_plot: the interactive plot     # - points(): a reactive object containing the user-drawn points as a tibble       # Render the widget in the UI placeholder   output$widget_ui <- renderUI({     res$youdrawit_plot   })      # observeEvent() watches res$points(), the reactive object   # Once the user completes their drawing, res$points() is populated   observeEvent(res$points(), {     # Because points is reactive, call res$points() inside reactive code     stopApp(res$points())  # stops the app and outputs the tibble of points   }) }   # runApp() launches the app and returns the user-drawn points once complete points <- runApp(shinyApp(ui, server)) # A tibble: 4 x 2       x     y   <dbl> <dbl> 1  10.0  15.2 2  10.5  15.4 3  11.0  15.8 4  11.5  16.1

title: Examples: sketchit()
text: sketchit() designed give users freeform drawing experience. Unlike drawit(), require one--one match drawing underlying data. Users can draw multiple lines, number lines can controlled within function. vignette, demonstrate sketchit() variety settings highlight several options customizing user experience.

title: Examples: sketchit()
text: geom_point geom_smooth geom_line , pass plot sketchit(), either pipeline saving object first Reset button clears drawings Undo button removes recently drawn line Done button locks drawing can longer edited color buttons allow users switch drawing colors

title: Examples: sketchit()
text: sketchit() data-matching requirements drawit(), works well wide range datasets. Let’s start high-density dataset palmerpenguins. Now compare lower-density dataset mtcars. One advantage sketchit() drawing experience remains largely regardless dense original data . contrast drawit(), user sketching freely rather drawing value x-position. also means output necessarily contain user-drawn y-value every x-value data. Like drawit(), sketchit() perfect replica ggplot2. However, preserve much styling information ggplot object passed .
code: (ggplot(data = penguins, aes(x = bill_length_mm, y = bill_depth_mm)) +   geom_point()) |>    sketchit() (ggplot(data = mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) +   geom_point()) |>    sketchit() (ggplot(data = penguins, aes(x = bill_length_mm, y = bill_depth_mm)) +   geom_point(color = "forestgreen") +    labs(title = "Sketchit Penguins Example",          subtitle = "Hope You Enjoy!",          x = "Bill Length (mm)",          y = "Bill Depth (mm)")     ) |>    sketchit()

title: Examples: sketchit()
text: One strengths sketchit() allows variety visual customizations can change overall drawing experience.

title: Examples: sketchit()
text: default, sketchit() includes palette “steelblue”, “orange”, “green”, “red”. can replace palette supplying values palette argument. Note first color palette used initial drawing color. can also change initial drawing color starting_color argument. color already included palette, added automatically. prefer users draw one color, can turn color controls entirely color_options = FALSE. works especially well combination starting_color.
code: (ggplot(data = mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) +     geom_line(size = 2) +     labs(title = "Sketchit Cars Example",          subtitle = "Hope You Enjoy!",          x = "Weight",          y = "Miles Per Gallon")     ) |>   sketchit(palette = c("red",                         "orange",                         "yellow",                         "green",                         "blue",                         "purple",                         "pink",                         "black")) #> Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0. #> ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead. #> This warning is displayed once per session. #> Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was #> generated. (ggplot(data = mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) +     geom_smooth(method = "loess") +     labs(title = "Sketchit Cars Example",          subtitle = "Hope You Enjoy!",          x = "Weight",          y = "Miles Per Gallon")     ) |>   sketchit(starting_color = "violet") #> `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x' (ggplot(data = penguins, aes(x = bill_length_mm, y = bill_depth_mm)) +   geom_point(color = "forestgreen") +    labs(title = "Sketchit Penguins Example",          subtitle = "Hope You Enjoy!",          x = "Bill Length (mm)",          y = "Bill Depth (mm)")     ) |>    sketchit(starting_color = "violet", color_options = FALSE)

title: Examples: sketchit()
text: Unlike drawit(), sketchit() limited two geoms. combination supported geoms can included plot. However, layers displayed immediately. currently way delay rendering individual layers.
code: (ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point(colour = "darkgreen") +    geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "blue") +    geom_line(color = "orange")+    ggtitle("This is a Title") +   labs(title = "Sketchit Cars Example",          subtitle = "Hope You Enjoy!",          x = "Weight",          y = "Miles Per Gallon")) |>    sketchit()

title: Examples: sketchit()
text: also possible increase width lines user draws stroke_width argument. Changing stroke width can noticeably affect drawing experience. Thicker lines can make sketches feel bolder easier see, thinner lines may feel precise.
code: (ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point(colour = "darkgreen") +    geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "blue") +    ggtitle("This is a Title") +   labs(title = "Sketchit Cars Example",          subtitle = "Hope You Enjoy!",          x = "Weight",          y = "Miles Per Gallon")) |>    sketchit(stroke_width = 5) #> `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

title: Examples: sketchit()
text: cases, drawing controls may overlap plotted data. can reposition control buttons button_position argument. argument accepts numeric vector length 2 values 0 1. default c(1, 1), places buttons top-right corner graph. contrast,c(0, 0) places bottom-left corner. example, c(0.5, 0.5) places controls center plot.
code: (ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point(colour = "darkgreen") +    ggtitle("This is a Title") +   labs(title = "Sketchit Cars Example",          subtitle = "Hope You Enjoy!",          x = "Weight",          y = "Miles Per Gallon")) |>    sketchit(button_position = c(0.5, 0.5))

title: Examples: sketchit()
text: using sketchit() graphical testing structured input tasks, may useful limit number lines user can draw. cases, may want require minimum number lines user allowed click Done. behaviors can controlled max_lines min_lines arguments. example, user must draw least two lines clicking Done, draw three lines total. can particularly useful Shiny applications testing settings number submitted sketches matters.
code: (ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point(colour = "darkgreen") +    ggtitle("This is a Title") +   labs(title = "Sketchit Cars Example",          subtitle = "Hope You Enjoy!",          x = "Weight",          y = "Miles Per Gallon")) |>    sketchit(max_lines = 3, min_lines = 2)

title: Examples: sketchit()
text: sketchit() function integrates directly Shiny, enabling interactive drawing inputs captured reused within reactive workflows. used inside Shiny application, sketchit() behaves differently static contexts. Instead returning r2d3 widget, returns list, contains: youdrawit_plot: interactive drawing widget points: reactive tibble containing user’s drawn data points object reactive() expression resolves tibble : x: user-drawn x-values y: user-drawn y-values color: color user-drawn line order: chronological order lines created (accounting undoes) line_id: identifier distinguishing separate lines enable communication browser Shiny server, must supply shiny_message_loc argument using sketchit() Shiny. argument defines input name used send drawn data browser back Shiny. drawing interaction handled JavaScript, Shiny runs R server, shiny_message_loc acts bridge two.

title: Examples: sketchit()
text: following example demonstrates minimal Shiny app captures user-drawn points returns tibble user finishes drawing (clicking Done), res$points() returns tibble containing recorded points user’s drawing. row corresponds one captured point, along information line belongs , order drawn (including undoes), selected color. might look something like : exact number rows depend user’s drawing. resulting x y values similar scale original dataset, allowing easy merges comparisons.
code: library(shiny)  # Define the User Interface (ui) ui <- fluidPage(   # Placeholder for the interactive drawit widget   uiOutput("widget_ui") )  # Define the Server Logic server <- function(input, output, session) {   # Create a ggplot object as the base plot for drawing   p <- ggplot(data = penguins, aes(x = bill_length_mm, y = bill_depth_mm)) +     geom_point(size = 2)      # This is the "bridge" between JavaScript (browser) and Shiny (R server)   shiny_message_loc <- "scatter_points"      # Initialize sketchit    res <- sketchit(     p, # the ggplot object     shiny_message_loc = shiny_message_loc     )   # res contains:     # - youdrawit_plot: the interactive plot     # - points(): a reactive object containing the user-drawn points as a tibble       # Render the widget in the UI placeholder   output$widget_ui <- renderUI({     res$youdrawit_plot   })      # observeEvent() watches res$points(), the reactive object   # Once the user completes their drawing, res$points() is populated   observeEvent(res$points(), {     # Because points is reactive, call res$points() inside reactive code     stopApp(res$points())  # stops the app and outputs the tibble of points   }) }   # runApp() launches the app and returns the user-drawn points once complete points <- runApp(shinyApp(ui, server)) # A tibble: 4 x 5       x     y color   order line_id   <dbl> <dbl> <chr>   <int>   <int> 1  10.0  15.2 steelblue   0       1 2  10.5  15.4 steelblue   0       1 3  11.0  15.8 red         4       2 4  11.5  16.1 red         4       2

title: You Draw It
text: ‘Draw ’ feature allows users interact chart directly drawing line computer screen mouse. Originally introduced New York Times 2015 purpose interactive reading, package adapts use ‘Draw ’ method tool interactive testing graphics.

title: You Draw It
text: can install development version youdrawitR GitHub :
code: #install.packages("pak") pak::pak("Alisa-Krasilnikov/youdrawitR")

title: You Draw It
text: functions can used geom_point, geom_smooth, geom_line. Note drawit() can used two geoms time, sketchit() supports multiple layers without restrictions.

title: You Draw It
text: drawit() allows users draw single continuous line across plot, enforcing one--one mapping x-values user-drawn y-values. particularly useful assessing users estimate regression lines expected trends. drawing region can customized, underlying data model can optionally revealed user completes drawing.
code: library(ggplot2) library(youdrawitR)  p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) +   geom_point(size = 1.5, colour = "pink") +   geom_smooth(method = "lm") +   labs(x = "Weight", y = "MPG")  drawit(p,         show_on_finish = TRUE,         draw_start = 3.5)

title: You Draw It
text: sketchit() enables flexible interaction, allowing users draw multiple lines without one--one constraint x y values. makes suitable exploratory open-ended tasks. Users can control number lines drawn (minimum maximum), customize visual properties color. See documentation available options.
code: library(ggplot2) library(youdrawitR)  p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) +   geom_point(size = 1.5, colour = "pink") +   geom_smooth(method = "lm") +   labs(x = "Weight", y = "MPG")  sketchit(p,         max_lines = 10,        min_lines = 2)

title: You Draw It
text: drawit() sketchit() can used within Shiny applications capture user input analysis. drawit() returns user-drawn y-values aligned original x-values ggplot2 dataset, preserving source data structure closely possible permitting minor smoothing. sketchit() returns detailed drawing data, including x y coordinates line segment, along attributes color drawing order. outputs can accessed via Shiny inputs used downstream tasks, comparing user predictions model results analyzing interaction behavior. ⭐ page minimal description package usability. highly recommend looking Articles section, vignettes examples go depth. ⭐

title: You Draw It
text: package active development. Features APIs may change project evolves. encounter issues suggestions, please open issue : https://github.com/Alisa-Krasilnikov/youdrawitR/issues

what: Interactive drawing on ggplot2 visualizations using D3 — drawit
title: Interactive drawing on ggplot2 visualizations using D3 — drawit
text: drawit function allows user draw single, continuous, one--one line. particularly useful tasks user expected estimate trends sketch expected relationships data.

title: Interactive drawing on ggplot2 visualizations using D3 — drawit
code: drawit(   p,   ...,   width = NULL,   height = NULL,   shiny_message_loc = NULL,   show_on_finish = FALSE,   draw_start = NULL,   smoother = 0.001 )

title: Interactive drawing on ggplot2 visualizations using D3 — drawit
text: p ggplot2 object. ... Additional arguments passed width Width output widget pixels. height Height output widget pixels. shiny_message_loc Name Shiny input/output binding used messaging. show_on_finish TRUE, displays secondary geom user finishes drawing. Note always second geom layer within ggplot2 object. draw_start Specifies drawing interaction begins, scale data. smoother number greater 0 controls much nearby x-values binned together. Larger values group points, creating smoother less detailed drawing (useful dense data). Values closer 0 keep points separate, preserving detail (useful exact x-y relationship matters)

title: Interactive drawing on ggplot2 visualizations using D3 — drawit
text: r2d3 htmlwidget.

title: Interactive drawing on ggplot2 visualizations using D3 — drawit
text: flexible, free-form drawing, see sketchit(). Currently, drawit can support geom_scatter, geom_line, geom_smooth, maximum two geoms per plot. highlighted region indicates drawing permitted. Users draw left right, interface provides visual feedback portions data range yet covered. drawing resolution tied x-values data. result, extending x-axis far beyond observed data without adjusting draw_start may lead overly granular drawing experience

title: Interactive drawing on ggplot2 visualizations using D3 — drawit
text: used within shiny application, widget returns recorded drawing data structured object user filled drawable region. x-value original data paired corresponding user-drawn y-value. returned data includes: x: x-values drawn user, preserving original data much possible y: user-drawn y-values corresponding x drawit() enforces single continuous line, output represents fully specified function domain data.

title: Interactive drawing on ggplot2 visualizations using D3 — drawit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Basic usage p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) +   geom_point(size = 2, colour = "magenta") +   labs(x = "Weight", y = "MPG")  drawit(p)  # Pipe-friendly usage (ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) +   geom_point(size = 2, colour = "magenta") +   labs(x = "Weight", y = "MPG")) |>   drawit() } # }

what: Extract ggplot2 data for D3 rendering — ggplot_youdrawit_payload
title: Extract ggplot2 data for D3 rendering — ggplot_youdrawit_payload
text: Takes ggplot2 object extracts underlying data layout information required re-render plot using D3. function processes output ggplot2::ggplot_build() obtain scaled data, panel information, aesthetic mappings.

title: Extract ggplot2 data for D3 rendering — ggplot_youdrawit_payload
code: ggplot_youdrawit_payload(p)

title: Extract ggplot2 data for D3 rendering — ggplot_youdrawit_payload
text: p ggplot2 object.

title: Extract ggplot2 data for D3 rendering — ggplot_youdrawit_payload
text: data frame containing processed data needed render plot D3, including scaled x y values aesthetic attributes.

title: Extract ggplot2 data for D3 rendering — ggplot_youdrawit_payload
text: function primarily intended internal use within youdrawit workflow. extracts post-scale data ggplot_build(), meaning transformations already applied. resulting structure used input payload downstream rendering functions drawit() sketchit().

title: Extract ggplot2 data for D3 rendering — ggplot_youdrawit_payload
code: library(ggplot2)  p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) +   geom_point(size = 2, colour = "magenta") +   labs(x = "Weight", y = "MPG")  ggplot_youdrawit_payload(p) #> $layers #> $layers[[1]] what: Free-form drawing on ggplot2 visualizations using D3 — sketchit
title: Free-form drawing on ggplot2 visualizations using D3 — sketchit
text: sketchit function enables flexible, free-form drawing ggplot2 visualization. Unlike drawit(), restricts users single continuous line, sketchit() allows multiple lines, color selection, greater control drawing behavior. makes suitable exploratory tasks, annotation, situations users may wish sketch multiple patterns highlight different features data.

title: Free-form drawing on ggplot2 visualizations using D3 — sketchit
code: sketchit(   p,   ...,   width = NULL,   height = NULL,   shiny_message_loc = NULL,   color_options = TRUE,   starting_color = NULL,   palette = NULL,   stroke_width = 2,   button_position = c(1, 1),   max_lines = NULL,   min_lines = NULL )

title: Free-form drawing on ggplot2 visualizations using D3 — sketchit
text: p ggplot2 object. ... Additional arguments passed width Width output widget pixels. height Height output widget pixels. shiny_message_loc Name Shiny input/output binding used messaging. color_options TRUE, enables multiple drawing colors. starting_color Initial drawing color. palette Set colors available drawing character vector. stroke_width Width drawn lines. button_position Position control interface within plot. Note numeric vector length 2, acceptable values 0 1. c(1,1) top right graph, c(0,0) bottom left. max_lines Maximum number lines user can draw. min_lines Minimum number lines required user can click "Done".

title: Free-form drawing on ggplot2 visualizations using D3 — sketchit
text: r2d3 htmlwidget.

title: Free-form drawing on ggplot2 visualizations using D3 — sketchit
text: Currently, sketchit can support geom_scatter, geom_line, geom_smooth. color_options = TRUE, default color pallete "steelblue", "orange", "green", "red". starting_color updated, starting_color value added pallete, assuming turned . palette updated, starting_color first color within palette.

title: Free-form drawing on ggplot2 visualizations using D3 — sketchit
text: used within shiny application, widget returns recorded drawing data structured object user clicks "Done" button. recorded user data includes: x y: coordinates point along drawn line color: color used draw line line_id: identifier distinguishing separate lines order: chronological order lines created line_id variable groups observations belonging drawn line, order reflects historical sequence drawing actions. Importantly, order reset lines removed. example, user draws blue line, red line, removes red line, finally draws green line, resulting identifiers may : Blue line: line_id = 1, order = 0 Green line: line_id = 2, order = 2 behavior preserves full interaction history allows downstream reconstruction analysis user actions.

title: Free-form drawing on ggplot2 visualizations using D3 — sketchit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Basic usage p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) +   geom_point()  sketchit(p)  # Custom colors and multiple lines sketchit(   p,   color_options = TRUE,   palette = c("red", "blue", "green"),   max_lines = 3 )  # Pipe-friendly usage (ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) +   geom_point()) |>  sketchit(palette = c("red", "blue", "green"),           max_lines = 3) } # }
